key_insight: Uniformly sampled matrices from a high-dimensional spectral norm ball become nearly "all-singular-values-equal-to-1" as dimension grows.
engineering_relevance: This supports a simple practical sampler for large matrix sizes, making spectral-norm-constrained initialization or perturbation more feasible in modern machine learning systems.
method: Prove asymptotic behavior of singular values for uniform samples from the spectral norm ball.
Use this limit result to justify a simpler approximate sampling procedure for large dimensions.
Compare the proposed approximation against exact sampling in experiments.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experimental results showing both singular-value convergence and agreement between exact and approximate sampling methods.
watch_item: The approximation is justified asymptotically, so small or moderate matrix sizes may deviate from the limiting behavior.
paper_title: Uniform Sampling from High-dimensional Spectral Norm Balls
